creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jessica Clarke from comment #14)
Yeah, I found that out too after some searching. The diff is going to be quite huge though, and I'm not sure if upstream would want that... :)

The whole thing with defining static_assert as a C preprocessor macro is rather iffy to begin with, and if we can ditch it, so much for the better.

That said, I agree with Warner that we should just drop the whole piece that attempts to do the _Static_assert via __COUNTER__. I had already tried replacing the line:

#elif __GNUC_PREREQ__(4,6) && !defined(__cplusplus)

with:

#elif __GNUC_PREREQ__(4,6) || defined(__clang__)

but maybe it's better to drop the whole second block.
